role_summary: Civil Rights History Project interviewee; co-subject of a joint oral history with Joseph Howell (catalog entry 67).
ai_reading: Embry Howell shares the embedding signature of the joint oral history at entry 67 (catalog slug 'joseph-howell-and-embry-howell'); see that catalog page for the substrate-derived neighbor cluster and axis observations the embedding produces for the combined transcript. Individual cosines for each named subject of a joint interview are not separable from the precomputed substrate, since the LoC transcript was recorded as a single audio file with multiple voices interleaved.
biographical_paragraph: Embry Howell is one of the named subjects of the joint Civil Rights History Project oral history at entry 67 (American Folklife Center accession AFC 2010/039) [src: 1]. This catalog page exists as a per-person index for Embry Howell; the substrate signature lives on the parent joint-interview page (see the AI's reading above) since the LoC transcript treats the joint conversation as a single recording. Embry Howell's Civil Rights History Project oral history is cross-referenced against the Library of Congress published transcript.

sources.title: Joint oral history at entry 67
sources.publisher: Civil Rights History Project, Library of Congress (AFC 2010/039)

gallery.alt: Chambers Building, Davidson College, Davidson, North Carolina
gallery.caption: The Chambers Building at Davidson College in Davidson, North Carolina, where Howell grew up and studied before the Southwest Georgia movement work recalled in her oral history.
